description_long: This object consists of a group of five mid-rise apartment buildings, each with a rectangular footprint and flat roofs. The buildings vary in height and color, with facades featuring regularly spaced windows and subtle architectural detailing such as cornices and window frames. The exteriors are finished in muted tones of beige, gray, and red, giving a classic urban residential appearance. The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns, and the buildings are closely aligned side by side, suggesting a dense city block. The structures appear to be constructed primarily from masonry or concrete, with some metallic and glass elements for windows and possible balcony railings. The overall design evokes early 20th-century urban architecture, commonly found in older neighborhoods of large cities.
description: A group of five mid-rise, rectangular apartment buildings with flat roofs, muted colors, and symmetrical window arrangements, evoking classic urban residential architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five buildings side by side, highlighting their varying heights, colors, and the regular window patterns.
description_view_1: Side view focusing on the tallest building, revealing the depth of the structures and the stacked floor arrangement.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the buildings, with similar window arrangements and flat roofs visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the alignment of the buildings and the differences in their heights and facade details.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in symmetrical vertical columns with even spacing between them, creating a uniform and orderly facade across all buildings.
